title: Baby Sleep Hours Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Recommended sleep hours for babies and infants by age. Find out how much sleep your baby needs.
intro: Raising babies and supporting family wellness requires planning across multiple areas: health, nutrition, development, and quality time together. Each stage—from newborn to toddler—has distinct sleep needs and milestones. Our calculator shows recommended sleep hours by age, with data updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you current.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **pediatric guidelines**. Direct calculation based on your baby's age. Recommended sleep hours by age.
useCases: Track your baby's sleep needs by age
Verify sleep recommendations before adjusting routines
Quick reference guide for caregivers and parents
seoKeywords: how much sleep does baby need
explanation:
## How Your Baby's Sleep Needs Change

Sleep is essential to your baby's growth and development. This calculator shows recommended sleep hours based on pediatric guidelines for each age.

## How It Works

The calculator applies sleep guidelines based on your baby's age in months. It provides ranges for total daily sleep, typical naps, and nighttime sleep, with alerts if values are outside normal ranges.

## Important Note

This calculator is a reference guide only. For medical concerns about your baby's sleep, consult your pediatrician or healthcare provider. Data is updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much should a newborn sleep per day?
faq.a: Newborns typically need 16-17 hours of sleep per day.

faq.q: How many hours does a 3-month-old baby need?
faq.a: At 3 months, babies need about 15 hours total, with 4-5 naps throughout the day.

faq.q: How much sleep does a 6-month-old need?
faq.a: 6-month-olds need 14-15 hours of sleep, typically with 2-3 naps.

faq.q: How much sleep should a 12-month-old get?
faq.a: At 12 months, babies need 13-14 hours, usually with 1-2 naps.
Toddlers at 2 years need 12-13 hours of sleep total, often with 1 nap.

faq.q: Is nap time still important for 3-year-olds?
faq.a: At 3 years, children need 11-12 hours; naps are optional but beneficial.

faq.q: What are signs my baby is tired?
faq.a: Watch for yawning, eye rubbing, fussiness, and decreased interest in play.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free and require no registration.
